purpose: Translate low-knowledge visual requests into concrete Minecraft implementation evidence chains instead of generic renderer or JSON advice.
evidenceOrder: workspace runtime facts: Minecraft version, loader, source roots, resource roots
local source or scripts: registry declarations, client init, renderer/screen bindings, sync paths
resource-pack assets: blockstates, models, textures, atlases, lang, font, particles, sounds, shaders, GUI textures
mod archive content: assets, data, nested JarJar content, owner classes
LSP, Gradle, source archive, or local jar evidence for loader/version APIs
versioned docs only after local evidence is insufficient
hardRules: Do not treat blockstate variants as runtime animation when visual state is continuous or high-cardinality.
Do not create textures, buffers, models, parse JSON, or send network packets every render frame.
Do not put client-only classes on dedicated-server load paths.
Do not invent renderer or screen code without checking registry id, client binding, asset path, sync evidence, and loader/version API proof.
Do not mix UI layout ownership, render pipeline state, and shader/post-processing ownership in one unbounded class.
Do not hard-code old GUI or rendering class names from memory after major version changes; search by role-equivalent API evidence.
requiredTraces: item definition or item model -> model
atlas -> sprite source directory or explicit file
registry id -> expected asset namespace and path
apiProofFields: API surface: renderer binding, screen binding, model layer, reload listener, dynamic texture, packet/sync
client visual surface: UI layout, render pipeline, shader/post-chain, asset references
source proof: file, type declaration, dependency source, local jar/source archive, LSP result, or versioned docs
mismatch risk when evidence appears to belong to another loader or version

assetRelations: texture -> atlas or sprite source when indirect
mustNotAssume: client display can mutate server-authoritative state

entryEvidence: pack metadata or runtime pack source
assetRelations: item definition or item model -> model
mustNotAssume: namespace normalization is correct without path proof
texture is on an atlas without atlas evidence

entryEvidence: reload listener or resource reload owner
assetRelations: shader json -> vertex or fragment program
mustNotAssume: external shader source is compatible Minecraft code
old shader class names still exist
uniforms are available without consumer proof

entryEvidence: pose stack or draw context usage
assetRelations: renderer -> model layer or baked model
server state -> client sync -> renderer state
mustNotAssume: render state can be read from server-only objects

evidenceChainContract.missingEvidenceBehavior: Return missing links and next reads before implementation code. If source, asset, or API proof is missing, downgrade from implementation to verification plan.

follow: atlas and font providers when texture use is indirect

reportAsConflict: registry namespace and asset namespace mismatch
asset path belongs to another loader or version

relationshipDiscoveryRules.externalReferenceRule: Use external shader references only for compact formulas or concepts, then map them to local uniforms, samplers, render targets, reload, and fallback evidence.

publicPayloadMustNotContain: screenshots or texture previews from private packs
redactionRules: strip private modpack names unless user approves
quote only minimal snippets from source or docs
summarize external shader formulas instead of copying code

shaderReferencePolicy.externalReference: Optional. Prefer a configured API key when activated. If unavailable, a local browser fallback may be used only to summarize formulas and concepts.
shaderReferencePolicy.sourcePolicy: Never depend on copied external shader source. Map compact formulas to local Minecraft uniforms, samplers, render targets, reload lifecycle, and fallback behavior.

outputExpectation: Return a compact evidence packet with proven links, missing links, next reads, and risk classification before proposing implementation code.
